description_long: This is a large, traditional church building with a steeply pitched, dark roof and a prominent central spire rising above the main entrance. The structure is primarily constructed from reddish-brown brick with detailed buttresses and arched windows along the sides, some of which appear to be filled with stained glass. The front facade features a large circular window, ornate stonework, and a set of double doors, flanked by smaller architectural details. The roof is adorned with crosses at the peak of the spire and at the ends of the roof ridges, emphasizing its religious function. A secondary, lower-roofed section extends from one side, likely serving as a transept or chapel, and the building sits on a rectangular base that suggests a landscaped or paved area around it. The overall design is reminiscent of Gothic Revival architecture, with pointed arches, vertical emphasis, and decorative stone elements.
description: A large, brick Gothic Revival church with a tall central spire, arched stained glass windows, and ornate stonework, set on a rectangular base.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entrance with double doors, a large circular window above, and the tall central spire rising from the roof.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the long nave, rows of arched windows, and the lower-roofed transept extending from the main structure.
description_view_2: The rear three-quarter view emphasizes the detailed buttresses, the length of the nave, and the spire's prominence.
description_view_3: The opposite side view displays the symmetrical arrangement of windows and buttresses, as well as the secondary roof section.
